sp.p: (aside) How shall I begin? (moves down stealthily; L.)

sp.speaker: Lester.
sp.p: She's here. (turns—starts) Edith! Some trick of the brain! (she hides her face in her hands) Pshaw! mere childishness! (by a sudden impulse he runs to her, and pulls her hands from her face)

sp.speaker: Edith.
sp.p: (pretending to burst out laughing at him) Is your little Antoine so well disguised?

sp.speaker: Lester.
sp.p: Antoine! (stupefied)

sp.speaker: Edith.
sp.p: Did you not tell me to dress as a girl to deceive your friend? (he still clasps her) Oh, let me go!

sp.speaker: Lester.
sp.p: Antoine! you! 'tis impossible. Those soft eyes that I have seen so sad. (as if trying to recall some memory)

sp.speaker: Edith.
sp.p: Sad! (laughs)

sp.speaker: Lester.
sp.p: No, no—'tis Edith!

sp.speaker: Edith.
sp.p: I?

sp.speaker: Lester.
sp.p: I am sure and then the likeness! Oh, what a blind idiot I have been! Pardon! (kneels)

sp.speaker: Edith.
sp.p: But I ––(confused and trembling)

sp.speaker: Lester.
sp.p: Oh, yes! speak to me as you did two years ago; for it all wildly returns now. Speak to me –I cannot be mistaken.

sp.speaker: Edith.
sp.p: (recovering her self-possession) Leave me!

sp.speaker: Lester.
sp.p: Tell me –tell me that you are Edith—my Edith! (holding her)

sp.speaker: Edith.
sp.p: Leave me! (she disengages herself from him)

sp.speaker: Lester.
sp.p: I know it—you are.

sp.speaker: Edith.
sp.p: (with entire self-possession) I am Edith—Edith Belton! and what can she be to you now? There was a time when you clasped me in your arms, and swore that nothing should divide us! (he makes a movement) No. (motions him to remain still) You chose to believe some lying story instead of my truth.

sp.speaker: Lester.
sp.p: They told me ––

sp.speaker: Edith.
sp.p: Aye! as they told me –that you were heartless, callous, and cared for some one else ; they sent back your letters, I refused to believe them ; but when I found that you did not come –that you did not make one effort to see me—then– then I thought my heart would have broken.

sp.speaker: Lester.
sp.p: Edith!

sp.speaker: Edith.
sp.p: Your friend told you, "One soon gets over these things." I accidentally heard that. And that "You may have found metal more attractive." I have seen that. So now, Edward Lester, we are both of us free.

sp.speaker: Lester.
sp.p: Free! I am free, free still to choose when I will. I have never forgotten you; never ceased to love you. What was it that drew me towards the peasant boy Antoine, but his resemblance to you? I have been culpable, selfish, capricious, but my first and only love is for you.

sp.speaker: Edith.
sp.p: And Lady Mosthyn? (Lester is silent – aside) He is deceiving me. (aloud) I see now the worth of your fair speeches. (going)

sp.speaker: Lester.
sp.p: (detaining her) No! on my soul. I do not care for her. Nay, I can hate her if she divides me from you.

sp.speaker: Edith.
sp.p: Go then. I shall never love again.

sp.speaker: Lester.
sp.p: Never! You cannot for my one foolish rash act, contemplated not committed, condemn both of us to unhappiness!

sp.speaker: Edith.
sp.p: (aside) If she should come!

sp.speaker: Lester.
sp.p: Here is my hand, Edith. You lead me to honour, she to disgrace. Save me, as my good angel, save me. (pause—he rises from his knees and goes R) Edith, Edith, you have no heart, you never could have loved me!

sp.speaker: Edith.
sp.p: I!

sp.speaker: Lester.
sp.p: Guilty as Lady Mosthyn may be, remember that for me she sacrifices wealth, husband's position everything! Yes, we will part. Farewell! (the clock slowly strikes the hour)

sp.speaker: Edith.
sp.p: (aside) To her. Oh! if he is true.

sp.speaker: Lester.
sp.p: (watching Edith, and looking from landing place at back—a boat very slowly and gradually approaches) She comes! The boat, my fate slowly glides towards us. It stops another moment, and she is upon the landing place. Now I am hers—

sp.speaker: Edith.
sp.p: Hers! never! (springing towards him)

sp.speaker: Lester.
sp.p: (catching her in his arms) Ah! then you do love me?

sp.speaker: Edith.
sp.p: I do. (falls into his arms)

div.stage: Madame Berliot from the boat appears on the landing place, in an attitude of astonishment

sp.speaker: Mad. B.
sp.p: Mon Dieu! vat do I see? Enter Feversham and Amy, Grigson, Mary and Watchitt, at back. Mr. Lestaire and—

sp.speaker: Lester.
sp.p: (L. C., presenting Edith) Miss Edith Belton, to whom I have been engaged for the last two years.

sp.speaker: Fever.
sp.p: (R. C.) Miss Amy Leigh, with similar accompaniment.

sp.speaker: Grigson.
sp.p: Halso Miss Mary Crick has hisn't hany longer to be.

sp.speaker: Watch.
sp.p: (R., coming forward) Exactly! then here you all are as I've been looking for. Oh! it's all right, I'm Mr. Watchitt of the Secret Enquiry Office. Lor' bless you, I find anything out, (aside) when other people give me the information.

sp.speaker: Mad. B.
sp.p: Madame Belton send you to look after dese young ladies.

sp.speaker: Watch.
sp.p: Which I will, mum, like a mother.

sp.speaker: Mad. B.
sp.p: You vill not be vanted, Mr. Washitt, as I, myself, take charge of dese—my nieces.

sp.speaker: Watch.
sp.p: Nieces! Then you are—

sp.speaker: Mad. B.
sp.p: Dere aunt—Mrs. Franks. (the two girls cross over)

sp.speaker: Grigson.
sp.p: Hallo! 'arf my property goes. Hi say, you'll 'ave to prove you were born, you know—I don't believe yer were.

sp.speaker: Mad. B.
sp.p: Oh! I make no claim of dat. I vant not ze money.

sp.speaker: Grigson.
sp.p: That, mum's a sentiment as do honor to your 'ead and 'art.

div.stage: Enter, L., Sir Thomas Mosthyn—Lester goes up.

sp.speaker: Sir Thom.
sp.p: (aside) They're gone off by this time. (aloud) Madame, (she comes to him) have you seen Lady Mosthyn?

sp.speaker: Mad. B.
sp.p: Oh! Sir Thomas, I've been looking for you everywhere—and I tink dat– (scream)

sp.speaker: Leslie.
sp.p: In the arbour.

div.stage: Feversham opens door and Marks with his back towards Sir Thomas, brings Lady Mosthyn forward

sp.speaker: All.
sp.p: Lady Mosthyn!

div.stage: Sir Thomas takes a step towards the arbour—Marks turns round

sp.speaker: Sir Thom.
sp.p: (seeing him) Marks!

div.stage: (Marks taking advantage of his stupefied attitude, clasps the handcuff on his wrist, and the pair are thus pinioned together )

sp.speaker: Marks.
sp.p: That's the way we join 'em in holy matrimony.

sp.speaker: Mad. B.
sp.p: But vat dis mean?

sp.speaker: Marks.
sp.p: (R.) Mean! Swindlers both on'em; charge of forgery. Settler this time. This 'ere's the Countess alias Sally Snaggs, and this 'ere's the artfullest old card in the pack, the Old Beau alias Doddle Snaggs. You've 'ad a narrow escape, Mr. Lester. Now, Watchitt, my'boy, bear a hand up to the trap.

sp.speaker: Sir Thom.
sp.p: (sneakingly) Come on.

sp.speaker: Lady M.
sp.p: (R.) Good-bye, Mr. Grigson; don't fatigue yourself at too many pic-nics. Adieu, Madame Berliot ; you'll have nobody's window blind to watch in your highly respectable house. Good-bye, Mr. Lester, my dear Edward—ha, ha, ha! Perhaps you'll be able to paint my picture from memory. I hope Miss Edith will not resume male attire after she's married —ha, ha, ha! (to Sir Thomas sharply) Come!

div.stage: Exit Watchitt—then exit grandly, Lady Mosthyn, and Sir Thomas sneakingly– then Marks.

sp.speaker: Pierre.
sp.p: (L.) "A long farewell to all my greatness." Divine Williams.

sp.speaker: Mad. B.
sp.p: (C.) Oh, ze wretches! de character of my house is gone!

sp.speaker: Grigson.
sp.p: (L.) Lor bless you, mum, 'tain't nothing at all in a hotel. Why hi recollect ven I was –(Mary pinches his arm)

sp.speaker: Mad. B.
sp.p: Ah, but de name? Vot vill de people say?

sp.speaker: Edith.
sp.p: You'd better ask them, aunt.

sp.speaker: Mad. B.
sp.p: Eh, bien. (to Audience) Eh? (snuffs) I look round to my friends, vat do dey say? Dey say ve 'ave known you a long time, Madame Berliot, and you alvays do ze best to please an' satisfy all zat come to your house. I shall always try to deserve your kind word, and to keep up the character of this establishment, "The Chalet in the Valley ." (music)

div.stage: Fever. Madame B. Lester. Edith. Grigion. Hetty. Alfred. Emmy. Mary. Pierre. c. L. R.
Curtain.

div.trailer: Printed by Thomas Scott, Warwick Court, Holborn.
